title: Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)

infobox.value: 2008 Opening Ceremony Vs New Zealand Māori

(also known as the **Indigenous All Stars**[[1]](#cite_note-ABC16-10-09-1)) is a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") team that represents [Aboriginal Australians](/wiki/Aboriginal_Australians "Aboriginal Australians") and [Torres Strait Islanders](/wiki/Torres_Strait_Islanders "Torres Strait Islanders"). The team was first formed in 1973 and currently plays in an annual [All Stars Match](/wiki/All_Stars_Match "All Stars Match") against the [New Zealand Māori rugby league team](/wiki/New_Zealand_M%C4%81ori_rugby_league_team "New Zealand Māori rugby league team").

AAP (16 October 2009). ["Indigenous All Stars by name, and nature"](https://web.archive.org/web/20091020055136/http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2009/10/16/2716693.htm). ABC. Archived from [the original](http://www.abc.net.au/news/stories/2009/10/16/2716693.htm) on 20 October 2009. Retrieved 8 December 2009.

[AAP](/wiki/Australian_Associated_Press "Australian Associated Press") (3 November 2007). ["We need to follow footprints of Barcaldine"](http://www.theage.com.au/news/sport/we-need-to-follow-footprints-of-barcaldine/2007/11/02/1193619147898.html). *[The Age](/wiki/The_Age "The Age")*. Australia: [Fairfax Media](/wiki/Fairfax_Media "Fairfax Media").

Webster, Andrew (9 February 2013). ["Gorden Tallis' father Wally was a pioneer for indigenous players"](https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/gorden-tallis-father-wally-was-a-pioneer-for-indigenous-players/news-story/bc523f792fc82a1012cd7748cb2d88c2). *DailyTelegraph*. Nationwide News. news.com.au. Retrieved 16 October 2025.

[AAP](/wiki/Australian_Associated_Press "Australian Associated Press") (18 February 1973). ["Aboriginal team wins"](https://news.google.com/newspapers?id=-LIpAAAAIBAJ&sjid=W-UDAAAAIBAJ&pg=3423,6519452). *[The Sydney Morning Herald](/wiki/The_Sydney_Morning_Herald "The Sydney Morning Herald")*. Australia: [Fairfax Media](/wiki/Fairfax_Media "Fairfax Media"). Retrieved 10 August 2010.
Lubowitz, Nora. ["Indigenous v Maori All Stars games"](https://www.watchallblacks.com/new-zealand-maori-rugby-live/). *Australia Rugby*. 08/01/2024. Retrieved 9 January 2024.

Andrew Lucas. Retrieved 21 August 2018.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Rugby league team

This article is about the rugby league team. For the Australian rules football team, see [Indigenous All-Stars (Australian rules football)](/wiki/Indigenous_All-Stars_(Australian_rules_football) "Indigenous All-Stars (Australian rules football)").

Indigenous All Stars

The **Indigenous Australian rugby league team** (also known as the **Indigenous All Stars**[[1]](#cite_note-ABC16-10-09-1)) is a [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") team that represents [Aboriginal Australians](/wiki/Aboriginal_Australians "Aboriginal Australians") and [Torres Strait Islanders](/wiki/Torres_Strait_Islanders "Torres Strait Islanders"). The team was first formed in 1973 and currently plays in an annual [All Stars Match](/wiki/All_Stars_Match "All Stars Match") against the [New Zealand Māori rugby league team](/wiki/New_Zealand_M%C4%81ori_rugby_league_team "New Zealand Māori rugby league team").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Indigenous_All_Stars_(rugby_league)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

An Aboriginal v White Australian rugby league game was held in [Barcaldine](/wiki/Barcaldine,_Queensland "Barcaldine, Queensland") in Western Queensland in 1963. These 'Blacks v Whites' games continued annually until 1984.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 1971 there was an Aboriginal knock-out rugby league competition.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The first Australian Aboriginal rugby league team was assembled in 1973. The Aboriginal team managed to win seven of its nine matches in just 10 days. The team consisted of 34 players: 19 from NSW, 13 from Queensland and two from the Northern Territory. They went on to win games against Kiwi premiers Wellington Petone, the only all-Māori team & Auckland club & [Te Atatu](/wiki/Te_Atatu_Roosters "Te Atatu Roosters"), which the Aboriginal side won 17–13.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The side competed in the [Pacific Cup](/wiki/Pacific_Cup "Pacific Cup") in [1990](/wiki/1990_Pacific_Cup "1990 Pacific Cup"), [1992](/wiki/1992_Pacific_Cup_(rugby_league) "1992 Pacific Cup (rugby league)") and [1994](/wiki/1994_Pacific_Cup "1994 Pacific Cup"). During this period the team contained [New South Wales Rugby League](/wiki/New_South_Wales_Rugby_League "New South Wales Rugby League") first grade players such as [Ricky Walford](/wiki/Ricky_Walford "Ricky Walford"), [Graham Lyons](/wiki/Graham_Lyons "Graham Lyons"), [Ron Gibbs](/wiki/Ron_Gibbs "Ron Gibbs"), [Darrell Trindall](/wiki/Darrell_Trindall "Darrell Trindall"), [Paul Davis](/wiki/Paul_Davis_(rugby_league) "Paul Davis (rugby league)"), Wayne Alberts, [George Longbottom](/wiki/George_Longbottom "George Longbottom") and [Will Robinson](/wiki/Will_Robinson_(rugby_league) "Will Robinson (rugby league)").

In the post-[1999 NRL season](/wiki/1999_NRL_season "1999 NRL season") an Aboriginal side managed by [Arthur Beetson](/wiki/Arthur_Beetson "Arthur Beetson") defeated the [Papua New Guinea Kumuls](/wiki/Papua_New_Guinea_Kumuls "Papua New Guinea Kumuls") and the future rugby league [Immortal](/wiki/The_Immortals_(rugby_league) "The Immortals (rugby league)") was pushing for an [Australia Day](/wiki/Australia_Day "Australia Day") match against the [Australian national team](/wiki/Australian_Kangaroos "Australian Kangaroos").

On the same day as the All Stars announcement, the league also revealed the NRL Pre-Season Challenge would return in 2024, with the tournament kicking off on February 15 and running across two weekends.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In 2008 World Cup curtain raiser to the Australia vs. New Zealand match included an Indigenous Australian squad playing against New Zealand Māori which featured several prominent NRL players and rising stars, including [Chris Sandow](/wiki/Chris_Sandow "Chris Sandow"), [Wairangi Koopu](/wiki/Wairangi_Koopu "Wairangi Koopu"), [Preston Campbell](/wiki/Preston_Campbell "Preston Campbell"), [Shaun Kenny-Dowall](/wiki/Shaun_Kenny-Dowall "Shaun Kenny-Dowall") and [Carl Webb](/wiki/Carl_Webb "Carl Webb").

[Preston Campbell](/wiki/Preston_Campbell "Preston Campbell"), a highly respected Aboriginal rugby league player, was a driving force behind the setup, in 2010, of a curtain raising match between the Indigenous All Stars, and the NRL All Stars. The Indigenous All Stars played what seems likely become an annual match against the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") All Stars on 13 February 2010.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The squad was chosen in part by public vote. Aboriginal NRL player [Sid Domic](/wiki/Sid_Domic "Sid Domic")'s artistic ability led him to be selected from a field of six artists to design the Indigenous side's jersey, as well as [Johnathan Thurston](/wiki/Johnathan_Thurston "Johnathan Thurston")'s and [Jamie Soward](/wiki/Jamie_Soward "Jamie Soward")'s custom headgear, for the annual All Stars matches.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

2019 will see a new format of the Australian Indigenous All-Stars play the [New Zealand Māori](/wiki/New_Zealand_M%C4%81ori_rugby_league_team "New Zealand Māori rugby league team") at AAMI Park in Melbourne on 15 February. It will be part of an All-Stars double header with the women's teams to play the curtain-raiser Māori and indigenous teams to the men's game. With no All-Stars game in 2017 due to the [World Cup](/wiki/World_Cup_(Rugby_league) "World Cup (Rugby league)"), the NRL will be hoping the inclusion of the [New Zealand Māori](/wiki/New_Zealand_M%C4%81ori_rugby_league_team "New Zealand Māori rugby league team") side will spark popularity in the fixture. Four Indigenous players were eligible for both teams: [Dane Gagai](/wiki/Dane_Gagai "Dane Gagai"), [Reimis Smith](/wiki/Reimis_Smith "Reimis Smith"), [Josh Hoffman](/wiki/Josh_Hoffman "Josh Hoffman") and [Javid Bowen](/wiki/Javid_Bowen "Javid Bowen").[[8]](#cite_note-8)
